lede: A look at July 2 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Julia Grabher and Chad Henne, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2024 Hathras crowd crush
events.desc: A stampede during a religious event in Uttar Pradesh, India, leaves at least 121 people dead and 150 others injured.

events.title: Vicente Fox
events.desc: Vicente Fox Quesada is elected the first President of México from an opposition party, the Partido Acción Nacional, after more than 70 years of continuous rule by the Partido Revolucionario Institucional, in the 2000 Mexican general election.

events.title: Sivas
events.desc: A mob sets fire to the Hotel Madımak in Sivas, Turkey, where a Alevi cultural festival was taking place, killing 37 people.

events.title: Aeroflot Flight 2306
events.desc: Aeroflot Flight 2306 crashes while attempting an emergency landing at Syktyvkar Airport in Syktyvkar, in present-day Komi Republic, Russia, killing 54 people.

events.title: Civil rights movement
events.desc: Civil rights movement: U.S. President Lyndon B. Johnson signs the Civil Rights Act of 1964 meant to prohibit segregation in public places.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: U.S. President Warren G. Harding signs the Knox–Porter Resolution formally ending the war between the United States and Germany.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: On the second day of the Battle of Gettysburg - the Battle of Little Round Top takes place and results in a Union victory after the Confederate troops unsuccessfully try to assault the Union left flank.

events.title: American Revolution
events.desc: American Revolution: The Continental Congress adopts the Lee Resolution severing ties with the Kingdom of Great Britain, although the wording of the formal Declaration of Independence is not adopted until July 4.

events.title: Battle of Yamazaki
events.desc: Battle of Yamazaki: Toyotomi Hideyoshi defeats Akechi Mitsuhide.

events.title: Emperor Taizong of Tang
events.desc: Li Shimin, the future Emperor Taizong of Tang, ambushes and kills his rival brothers Li Yuanji and Li Jiancheng in the Xuanwu Gate Incident.

births.name: Kim Carr
births.line: Australian educator and politician, 31st Australian Minister for Human Services

births.name: Georgi Ivanov (cosmonaut)
births.line: Bulgarian military officer, cosmonaut and politician

births.name: William Henry Bragg
births.line: English physicist, chemist, and mathematician, Nobel Prize laureate (died 1942)

deaths.text: Emilio Álvarez Montalván, Nicaraguan ophthalmologist and politician
Natasha Shneider, Russian-American singer, keyboard player, and actress
Ernest Hemingway, American novelist, short story writer, and journalist, Nobel Prize laureate
Spencer Compton, 1st Earl of Wilmington, English politician, Prime Minister of the United Kingdom

observances.text: Christian feast day: Aberoh and Atom (Coptic Church)
Christian feast day: Feast of the Visitation (Anglicanism; Levoča at Mariánska hora)
